title: First Impact and the arrival of the seeds
display_title: First Impact and the arrival of the seeds
wikitext:
[]: First Impact and the arrival of the seeds             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Mon Oct 05, 2009 2:37 pm So, I was reading the latest stage of Sadamoto's manga, in which Adam already was on Earth (as in Earth came with Adam included) and Lilith arrives later on First Impact. And then I started asking myself how this First Impact thing was in the original Eva. I mean, IIRC both seeds came from outside, Adam fell first and then Lilith. But there was only one First Impact. How the hell did this all work out in Eva the original? With all this Rebuild shit and stuff I'm starting to forget all this.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Mon Oct 05, 2009 2:45 pm First Impact was the formation of the moon, and Lilith making that. Presumably Adam arrived in a way that was less destructive because ve was supposed to arrive there.

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Mon Oct 05, 2009 4:09 pm The FAR, most likely, gently set Adam down on Earth. But, Lilith was thrown down to earth from somewere else. I also wonder what happened to her original homeworld?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Oct 05, 2009 4:10 pm Pretty much. The Confidential Information goes out of it's way to say that Lilith had an extra violent landing that totally wrecked Earth's shit.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Mon Oct 05, 2009 4:11 pm Holy Diver wrote: I also wonder what happened to her original homeworld?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Oct 05, 2009 4:38 pm Holy Diver wrote: I also wonder what happened to her original homeworld?

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Mon Oct 05, 2009 4:48 pm Lilith = Giant Naked Dojikko?

[]:             Quote Post by User-iel » Tue Oct 06, 2009 3:27 am Gee, and to think the FAR gave us bocce and curling? But try reading Impact == Extinction

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Oct 06, 2009 12:52 pm LiLi wrote: Lilith = Giant Naked Dojikko?

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Tue Oct 06, 2009 1:05 pm ^Mind your language or I'll send GNK to spank you!

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Tue Oct 06, 2009 4:27 pm Sachi wrote: Lilith was probably being sent at high speed to get to whichever planet she was supposed to land on, but Earth got in the way of the trajectory and she crash landed there.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Oct 06, 2009 7:10 pm More likely, they have to navigate to the planets themselves, or they're programmed to land on the "nearest Goldilocks zone planet" or something.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Tue Oct 06, 2009 7:13 pm Also possible: Since the FAR seem to let this happen autonomously, rather than custom-placing each seed, they released Lilith and Adam in the same general direction, with the Black Moon to be using the Sun in a gravity assist to another system. However, while they accurately predicted the existence of the larger planets, they missed Mars and Mercury, and Lilith's path was interfered with by the gravity of either or both of those two worlds, sending it on a path to Earth.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Oct 06, 2009 8:51 pm Or Lilith got hit by a large asteroid and knocked off course lightyears away from our solar system. Either way, one need not assume the FAR are at fault.

[]:             Quote Post by Teague » Wed Oct 07, 2009 4:57 am AuraTwilight wrote: LiLi wrote: Lilith = Giant Naked Dojikko?

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Wed Oct 07, 2009 5:02 am I'd appreciate not being called that. I mean it. Thanks. :EDIT: I was about to quote Kaworu on that one: Kaworu wrote: I really wish you wouldn't make statements when you lack evidence for them.

[]:             Quote Post by Chaos Pudding » Fri Oct 09, 2009 1:06 pm So Adam arrives first, and relatively gently. Then Lilith arrived with a bang, because she wasn't supposed to/didn't expect to land on Earth. Lilith's landing was 1st Impact, and it created our Moon. Then what about Adam? Was Adam already awake at the time that Lilith landed? In that case, did her Spear just up and skewer her through the back? According to what I can tell from the still that depicts when humans first found Adam, she was covered in snow, which would imply that she managed to make it out of the White Egg, at least. I mean, I suppose it's possible that there was snow inside the White Egg, but that seems to be improbable, considering that, apparently, the White Egg landed with less force than the Black Egg, and the Black Egg was pretty much pristine in regards to its outer shell. I'm kind of hoping that we get some sort of explanation in Rebuild of what really happened when Lilith landed. Maybe as some sort of prologue in front of 3.0? I mean, since it's Rebuild, it wouldn't necessarily depict what happened during the series, but it could give a clearer picture.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Fri Oct 09, 2009 1:09 pm Adam was awake and got speared. The White Egg didn't have snow in it naturally, it decayed over the years and when it ended up in Antarctica snow managed to get inside when humans excavated. The Black Egg probably wasn't pristine; the core got buried and the Geofront (which had to be excavated) was what was left of the outer shell. GNR just reformed it.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Fri Oct 09, 2009 1:54 pm 'tseems Adam was never awake until humans woke it (as stated in the CI) by meddling with the lance.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Oct 09, 2009 2:28 pm She doesn't have a homeworld. She wandered through space until she settled on Earth.

And then I started asking myself how this First Impact thing was in the original Eva. I mean, IIRC both seeds came from outside, Adam fell first and
